title: NVIDIA vs AMD: The GPU Revenue War (2024)

scenes.narration_text: NVIDIA and AMD have been battling for GPU dominance for decades. But in 2024, the revenue gap tells a very different story than the spec sheets.

data.heading: NVIDIA vs AMD: The Revenue War

scenes.narration_text: Let's start with the raw revenue numbers. NVIDIA pulled in 60.9 billion dollars in fiscal 2024, while AMD came in at 22.7 billion. Intel's GPU division barely registered at 1.5 billion.
But the real story is the stock performance. Look at how NVIDIA has absolutely crushed it over the past two years compared to AMD and Intel.

visual_instruction.title: NVDA vs AMD vs INTC — 2Y Stock Performance

data.subtitle: Percentage change over 2 years — NVIDIA's AI-driven surge

scenes.narration_text: Zooming into just NVIDIA's stock price over the past year, you can see the relentless climb driven by AI demand and data center revenue.
Where is all this NVIDIA money coming from? Data center revenue now makes up 78 percent of their total. Gaming, which used to be their bread and butter, is down to just 17 percent.

data.subtitle: Data Center dominance: 78% of total revenue

scenes.narration_text: And the semiconductor sector as a whole has been on a tear. Let's compare the big players — NVIDIA, AMD, Broadcom, and Qualcomm over the past year.
The bottom line? NVIDIA isn't just winning the GPU war. They've redefined what a GPU company even is. AMD is solid, but the AI wave has turned this into a completely different race.

data.heading: NVIDIA Redefined the Game
data.body: AI turned a GPU company into a $3T titan
